question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted request to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens with a maximally large `limit` parameter value, causing TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() to execute an unbounded SQL query that exhausts database connection pool resources, preventing the mirror node from processing and gossiping other incoming transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply simultaneous spender.id gte/lte and token.id gte/lte range filters to force extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to generate all three lower/inner/upper subqueries in a UNION ALL, causing a combinatorial SQL explosion that blocks the database and prevents token allowance transactions from being gossiped through the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens endpoint with high-concurrency requests using different valid account IDs, exhausting the Node.js async event loop and database connection pool, causing the mirror node to drop or delay processing of token allowance transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a valid EVM address as the idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter that resolves via EntityService.getEncodedId() to trigger a slow database lookup, and by sending many such requests in parallel, starve the connection pool and prevent token allowance transactions from being served or gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with both spender.id=gte:1&spender.id=lte:999999999 and token.id=gte:1&token.id=lte:999999999 to force getLowerFilters(), getInnerFilters(), and getUpperFilters() to each produce non-empty filter sets, generating a three-way UNION ALL query that scans the entire token_allowance table and prevents other transactions from being gossiped due to database lock contention?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use a valid account alias string as the idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter to trigger EntityService.getAccountIdFromAlias(), and by sending thousands of such requests with different alias values, cause repeated full-table alias lookups that saturate the database and suppress gossip of token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated requests with the maximum allowed repeated query parameters for spender.id (up to maxRepeatedQueryParameters), causing Bound.parse() to be called in a tight loop and the resulting multi-union SQL to contain deeply nested conditions, degrading database performance and preventing token allowance transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a non-existent but syntactically valid account ID (e.g., 0.0.999999999) to getAccountTokenAllowances(), causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to return a valid encoded ID that results in a full token_allowance table scan with no matching rows, and by repeating this at scale, prevent legitimate token allowance transaction data from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the pagination `next` link returned by getPaginationLink() by crafting a request where bounds.primary is empty and bounds.secondary is empty, causing the pagination logic to emit a next link that loops back to the same query indefinitely, enabling a bot to generate infinite recursive requests that suppress gossip of new token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user set order=desc and supply a spender.id=gte:X&token.id=gte:Y filter combination to force extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce a lower-only subquery with a descending ORDER BY, causing a full reverse index scan on the token_allowance table that blocks concurrent writes and prevents token allowance transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with limit=1 and a spender.id range that spans the entire ID space, causing TokenAllowanceService.getQuery() to generate a UNION ALL of three subqueries each with LIMIT 1, resulting in three separate full-range index scans that collectively suppress the mirror node's ability to gossip token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id=eq:X filter alongside a token.id=gte:Y&token.id=lte:Z range, causing getLowerFilters() to include all six filter slots (primary.equal, primary.lower, primary.upper, secondary.lower, secondary.equal, secondary.upper) in a single subquery, generating an overly complex WHERE clause that degrades query performance and prevents token allowance transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a syntactically valid but extremely long EVM address string as idOrAliasOrEvmAddress to trigger EntityService.getEntityIdFromEvmAddress(), causing a slow hex-to-buffer conversion and database lookup that, when repeated at scale, prevents the mirror node from gossiping token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with no filters at all, causing extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce a query with empty lower/inner/upper filters, which results in TokenAllowanceService.getSubQuery() generating a SELECT * FROM token_allowance WHERE owner=$1 AND amount>0 with only a LIMIT clause, performing a full owner-scoped table scan that, when sent at scale for many account IDs, prevents gossip of token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getAccountTokenAllowances() does not rate-limit or throttle requests per account ID, allowing them to send thousands of requests per second for a single high-value account ID, causing repeated expensive SQL queries that saturate the database and prevent token allowance transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply spender.id=gte:0 (minimum value) and token.id=gte:0 to force getInnerFilters() to produce a primary.lower filter with operator gt, generating a subquery that scans all token allowances with spender > 0, and by sending this at high frequency, prevent the mirror node from gossiping token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter is a valid account alias that matches multiple entities, causing EntityService.getAccountFromAlias() to throw an internal Error (not InvalidArgumentError), which propagates unhandled and crashes the request handler, preventing token allowance transaction gossip for that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply spender.id=lte:X and token.id=lte:Y to force getUpperFilters() to return [{...primary.upper, operator: eq}, secondary.upper], generating a subquery with spender=X AND token_id<=Y, and by sending this with X=maximum BigInt value, cause a full table scan that prevents token allowance transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests with different valid spender.id and token.id range combinations to getAccountTokenAllowances(), causing the database to execute multiple simultaneous UNION ALL queries that collectively hold shared locks on the token_allowance table, preventing writes from token allowance transactions and suppressing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum allowed limit value to extractTokenMultiUnionQuery(), causing TokenAllowanceService.getQuery() to set LIMIT to a very large number in each of the three UNION ALL subqueries, resulting in massive result sets that exhaust Node.js heap memory and prevent the mirror node from gossiping token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request deduplication in getAccountTokenAllowances() to send identical requests simultaneously, causing the database to execute the same expensive UNION ALL query multiple times in parallel, saturating I/O and preventing token allowance transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply spender.id=gte:1&spender.id=lte:1 (equal range) alongside token.id=gte:1&token.id=lte:1 to cause validateBoundsRange() to throw an InvalidArgumentError, and by sending thousands of such invalid requests, overwhelm the error-handling middleware and prevent legitimate token allowance transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a shard.realm.num format account ID with maximum shard and realm values (e.g., 9999.9999.9999999999) to getAccountTokenAllowances(), causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to compute an extremely large encoded ID that, when used in the SQL query, causes a full table scan and prevents token allowance transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with only token.id=eq:X (no spender.id), causing validateSecondaryBound() to throw an InvalidArgumentError, and by flooding the endpoint with such requests, overwhelm the error response pipeline and prevent the mirror node from gossiping token allowance transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() calls super.getRows() without a query timeout, by sending a request that generates a slow UNION ALL query, causing the database connection to be held indefinitely and preventing other token allowance transactions from being gossiped?
